title: JSONL files always render in dark mode regardless of theme setting
description:
## Symptom
Opening a .jsonl file in OpenMarkdownReader always displays with a dark background/theme, even when the app is set to light mode or system theme (and system is in light mode).

## Likely causes
- JSONL files hit the syntax-highlighted code path (via highlight.js) which may have its own theme stylesheet that doesn't respect the app's light/dark toggle
- The app loads both `vendor/github.min.css` (light) and `vendor/github-dark.min.css` (dark) for highlight.js — the wrong one may be active for non-markdown files
- JSON/JSONL content is likely wrapped in a `<pre><code>` block where the highlight.js theme overrides the app's CSS variables
- The theme switching logic in renderer.js may only toggle markdown-related styles, not the highlight.js code theme

## Investigation checklist
- [ ] Open a .jsonl file in light mode, inspect the `<pre><code>` element — which highlight.js stylesheet is active?
- [ ] Search renderer.js for theme switching logic — does it swap the highlight.js CSS file?
- [ ] Check if .json files have the same issue (same rendering path)
- [ ] Check styles.css for any `.hljs` overrides that force dark backgrounds

## Fix (likely)
Swap the highlight.js theme stylesheet when the app theme changes:
- Light mode: load `vendor/github.min.css`
- Dark mode: load `vendor/github-dark.min.css`
Or use CSS variables to override `.hljs` background/foreground to match the app theme.

title: Add document comments in view mode
Open folders on remote SSH hosts (SFTP-backed browsing/editing)
description:
## Goal
Let the user open a folder on a remote machine they're already SSH-connected to (e.g. `hostmyapple`, `macincloud`, `noos-prod`) and browse/read markdown files as if it were a local folder.

## Motivation
Jacob frequently SSHs into Mac mini (hostmyapple), Hetzner box (jcortex), and others — and often wants to read markdown notes or READMEs living on those boxes without scp'ing them down first.

## Feasibility: Yes
Several viable approaches, in order of preference:

### Option A — SFTP via `ssh2-sftp-client` (RECOMMENDED)
- npm: `ssh2-sftp-client` (wraps `ssh2`, pure JS with optional native crypto). Both are well-maintained.
- Parses `~/.ssh/config` via `ssh-config` package → lets user pick an existing Host alias (`hostmyapple`, `jcortex`, etc.) instead of retyping creds.
- Uses existing SSH keys — no password entry for key-based hosts.
- New IPC surface in main.js: `remote-fs:connect`, `remote-fs:list`, `remote-fs:read`, `remote-fs:stat`, `remote-fs:write` (for edit mode).
- File tree builds an in-memory `fileTree` just like local, but every node carries a `remoteId`.
- Renderer treats remote paths as a new scheme, e.g. `sftp://hostmyapple/Users/Jacob/notes/foo.md`.
- Cache small files locally (`~/Library/Application Support/OpenMarkdownReader/remote-cache/<host>/`) for offline re-open + diff.

### Option B — sshfs/macFUSE mount
- User mounts remote dir via macFUSE + sshfs; app just opens the mount point as a normal local folder.
- Zero code in OpenMarkdownReader, but requires user to install macFUSE (kext/system extension, macOS is actively hostile to this).
- Not a good default, but document as a workaround.

### Option C — Remote agent (VS Code style)
- Ship a tiny companion binary to the remote host, speak a custom protocol over SSH stdio.
- Overkill for a markdown reader. Skip.

## Proposed UX
- **File → Open Remote Folder…** menu item (new)
- Dialog shows hosts parsed from `~/.ssh/config`, plus a "Custom…" row
- After picking host, prompt for path (or show remote home dir browser)
- Opened remote folder appears in the sidebar with a small 🌐 or hostname badge
- Titlebar shows `hostmyapple:~/notes` so it's always clear which machine you're reading from
- Recent Remote Folders section in File menu (mirror of Recent Folders)

## Edge cases to consider
- **Edit mode + write-back**: save over SFTP, show spinner in tab, revert on failure
- **Large folders**: lazy-load subtrees instead of walking entire tree (unlike local mode)
- **File watching**: no easy SFTP notify; poll `mtime` on currently-open files every N seconds, or just refresh-on-focus
- **Wiki links & cross-file navigation**: all resolves must stay within the same remote root
- **Disconnects**: detect, show "reconnect" toast, queue writes or fail them
- **MAS sandbox**: SFTP will likely NOT work under App Sandbox (outbound network + arbitrary file access). Feature should be gated to the non-MAS / "Local-Only" edition, OR make it Pro/non-MAS only. Coordinate with `markdown-reader-xwc` (Local-Only MAS edition).
- **Security**: only read from `~/.ssh/config` + `~/.ssh/known_hosts`; never prompt for or store passwords in plaintext; rely on key agent (ssh-agent) where possible.

## Out of scope for v1
- Real-time collab on remote files
- Remote terminal inside the app
- Writing back to remote via git

## Acceptance criteria
1. "File → Open Remote Folder…" works against `hostmyapple`, showing markdown files in sidebar
2. Clicking a file opens it read-only in a new tab with correct rendering (including wiki links and images, at least for those inside the same remote root)
3. Edit mode saves back to the remote host and the tab's dirty indicator clears on success
4. Disconnecting/reconnecting doesn't crash the renderer (see existing dead-renderer guards)
5. Works against at least one non-Mac remote (Linux box like jcortex) to prove portability

## Related
- Blocks / interacts with: `markdown-reader-xwc` (Local-Only MAS edition) — remote support must live in the non-sandboxed build

title: Undo history is lost on mode toggle / tab switch / rich↔plain toggle
description:
**Problem**: Undo state is repeatedly wiped by normal UI actions. User types for 10 minutes, hits Escape to preview, goes back to edit → Cmd+Z does nothing.

**Three places undo is lost** (found in renderer.js):

1. **Edit → Preview → Edit (plain textarea mode)** — renderer.js:1212 'showEditor(content)' does 'editor.value = content' every time. Assigning .value to a textarea programmatically **resets the browser's native undo stack**. So any mode toggle clears undo.

2. **Rich mode ↔ Plain mode toggle** — renderer.js:5597-5602 'destroyRichEditor()' calls 'easyMDE.toTextArea()', which tears down the entire CodeMirror instance. All undo history gone. The reverse direction pipes via 'easyMDE.value(editor.value)' (line 5611) which also starts a fresh history.

3. **Tab switching** — likely same issue: returning to a tab re-runs 'showEditor(tab.content)' → textarea .value reassignment → undo wiped. Needs verification but the pattern is suspicious.

**Why this matters now**: The proposed Escape-to-exit-edit-mode feature (markdown-reader-kq3) would make this 10x more frequent. Mis-tapping Escape shouldn't lose 20 minutes of undo.

**Fix directions** (in order of effort):
- **Cheap**: In plain mode, don't reassign 'editor.value' if the content hasn't actually changed from what the user last saved or what's currently in the textarea. Mode toggle becomes pure show/hide.
- **Medium**: Persist **per-tab editor state** so that switching tabs (or modes) restores the textarea/CodeMirror instance rather than re-populating from content.
- **Right answer**: One editor instance per tab, kept alive across mode/tab changes. Bigger refactor but it's the correct model — matches VS Code/Obsidian/any real editor.

**Current escape hatch**: 'tab.originalContent' (renderer.js:1178) gives a 'revert all' via the Cancel button, but that's all-or-nothing — no granular undo.

**Acceptance**: After typing, hitting Escape/preview toggle, switching tabs, or toggling rich/plain mode, Cmd+Z should still step back through all edits in the session.
close_reason: Implemented per-tab editor refactor. Each tab now owns its own textarea (tab.editorEl) and EasyMDE instance (tab.easyMDE), both lazy-created and kept alive across tab switches and preview toggles. Verified end-to-end via the new _debug-editor-state agent command: setting distinct content in two tabs and switching between them preserves each tab's CodeMirror state independently. Preview-toggle round-trip also preserves the editor instance (no destroy/recreate). Within-tab rich/plain mode toggle still loses undo on the OLD mode — that's a separate, smaller concern documented in the new follow-up.

title: Prompt to become default handler for .md/.jsonl on first launch
description:
## Goal
On first launch (or when the app detects it's not the default handler), show a non-intrusive banner or dialog asking: "Make OpenMarkdownReader your default app for Markdown files?"

## Current state (2026-04-09)
- .md and .jsonl defaults set manually via `duti -s com.jacobcole.openmarkdownreader .md all`
- .jsonl added to fileAssociations in package.json (will be in Info.plist after next build)
- Stale "OpenMarkdownReader 2.app" (TestFlight wrapper) was poisoning LaunchServices; fixed by unregistering via lsregister

## Implementation sketch
1. On `app.whenReady()`, check if `app.isDefaultProtocolClient()` or equivalent for file types
2. Electron doesn't have a built-in "is default for file extension" check, so use `duti -x md` or `LSCopyDefaultRoleHandlerForContentType` via native module
3. If not default, show a subtle banner (not a modal) with "Set as default" / "Not now" / "Don't ask again"
4. Store preference in config: `defaultHandlerPromptDismissed: true`
5. Gate behind `!isMASBuild()` — MAS apps can't call `duti` or `lsregister`

## File types to check
- .md (primary)
- .markdown, .mdown, .mkd (variants)
- .jsonl (newly added)
- NOT .json, .txt, .yaml — too aggressive, users likely want VS Code/other for those

## UX reference
Similar to Chrome's "Make Chrome your default browser?" or VS Code's "Make VS Code your default editor?"

## Out of scope
- Auto-setting without asking (bad UX, user should consent)
- Setting defaults for ALL registered file types (only markdown family + jsonl)

title: Wire up electron-updater for non-MAS DMG auto-updates
description:
## Goal
Wire up `electron-updater` for the **non-MAS** DMG distribution so Jacob and other direct-download users automatically get new releases without manually downloading DMGs or re-building from source.

## Current state (verified 2026-04-08)
- `package.json` has **no `build.publish`** config
- **`electron-updater` is not a dependency**
- `main.js` never imports `autoUpdater`
- Result: v1.0.4 users who installed from GitHub are stuck on v1.0.4 forever unless they manually re-download

## Why this matters
- Currently my workflow to get the latest code running is "build locally, ditto into /Applications". That's fine for one user. Not fine for anyone else.
- The Local-Only MAS edition (markdown-reader-xwc) and MAS Standard get updates via Apple automatically. The non-MAS build is currently the odd one out with *no* update path at all.
- The non-MAS edition is strategically the most important one for power users (it's where SFTP, shell integration, and future power features will live — see markdown-reader-571). Power users especially need fast update cadence.

## Implementation

### 1. Add dependency
```bash
npm install electron-updater
```
**Dep version policy**: per CLAUDE.md, use a version at least one month old. Check npm before installing.

### 2. Configure publishing in `package.json`
```json
"build": {
  "publish": {
    "provider": "github",
    "owner": "tmad4000",
    "repo": "OpenMarkdownReader",
    "releaseType": "release"
  }
}
```

### 3. Wire updater into `main.js`
Gate on non-MAS build using the existing `isMASBuild()` helper — MAS updates are handled by Apple and calling `autoUpdater` from a MAS build will crash or no-op ugly:

```javascript
const { autoUpdater } = require('electron-updater');

app.whenReady().then(() => {
  // ... existing ready logic ...
  if (!isMASBuild()) {
    autoUpdater.checkForUpdatesAndNotify().catch(err => {
      console.warn('[auto-update] check failed:', err.message);
    });
    // Re-check every 6 hours for long-running instances
    setInterval(() => {
      autoUpdater.checkForUpdatesAndNotify().catch(() => {});
    }, 6 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
  }
});
```

### 4. UI: "Update available" indicator
Per global CLAUDE.md guidance ("Always show when a new version is available"), add a subtle indicator in the user menu / footer:
- Listen for `autoUpdater.on('update-available', ...)` → set a flag in main, forward to renderer via IPC
- Renderer shows a small dot next to the version number (already displayed per CLAUDE.md "Version Display in User Menus" rule)
- Clicking the dot opens a "Restart to update" dialog backed by `autoUpdater.quitAndInstall()`
- Listen for `autoUpdater.on('update-downloaded', ...)` → enable the restart option

### 5. Release workflow change
- electron-builder needs to publish `latest-mac.yml` alongside the DMG/zip in every GitHub release or the updater has nothing to check against
- Change release command from `gh release upload` → `electron-builder --mac --publish always`
- This requires `GH_TOKEN` env var in the shell doing the release (from 1Password or gh auth)
- Update `CLAUDE.md` "Releasing" section with the new command

### 6. Signing requirement
- electron-updater on macOS **requires** signed + notarized builds to apply updates (unsigned apps get "update failed: code signature" errors)
- This means going back to the notarized build flow (`APPLE_API_KEY` env vars) for every release — no more skip-notarize shortcuts for public releases
- Local dev installs can still skip notarization since they bypass the updater

## Acceptance criteria
1. Fresh v1.0.N install from GitHub DMG auto-detects and installs v1.0.N+1 without manual intervention
2. User sees "Update available" indicator before restart, not just a silent swap
3. MAS builds don't crash or get confused — `isMASBuild()` gate prevents any updater code from firing
4. Release script documented in CLAUDE.md and produces the `latest-mac.yml` file in the GitHub release
5. `autoUpdater` errors don't break app startup (wrapped in try/catch, logged not thrown)

## Edge cases
- **First run after install**: avoid checking for updates immediately on first launch — wait a few minutes so the UI loads first
- **Offline / no network**: updater should silently fail and not bother the user
- **Code signing identity rotates**: the new build must be signed with the *same* identity or the updater refuses. Document this.
- **Differential updates**: electron-updater supports delta patches but requires the previous build's signature. First few updates will be full downloads.

## Out of scope
- In-app update notes / changelog display (can be added later)
- Nightly / pre-release channel (the alpha group on TestFlight covers pre-release testers for now)

## Related
- Discovered during 2026-04-08 session after manually installing v1.0.4 DMG and realizing there's no update path

title: Far-future: Real-time collaborative editing via Yjs + Milkdown
description:
**Not urgent — parking ticket** to preserve the decision context from the 2026-04-02 session.

**Goal**: Eventually support real-time collaborative editing on markdown files (multiple users editing the same doc with live cursors and awareness).

**Approach**:
- Milkdown (ProseMirror-based) already has @milkdown/plugin-collab which wraps Yjs + y-prosemirror — the same stack Tiptap uses. Adding Milkdown as the editor (markdown-reader-r1r) is the enabling move; collab is a plugin on top.
- Tiptap has a more mature commercial collab story (Tiptap Cloud), but we're choosing Milkdown for other reasons. Collab capability on Milkdown is fine — just less polish on the DX.
- Yjs is the CRDT library. Transport options: y-websocket (self-hosted, simple), y-webrtc (P2P, NAT pain), y-indexeddb (local persistence).

**Hard problems to solve before shipping collab**:
1. **Backend/transport**: where does the Yjs server live? Self-host a tiny y-websocket server? Piggyback on noos / IdeaFlow backend? This decision is upstream of everything.
2. **On-disk reconciliation**: OpenMarkdownReader is a file-based editor. If someone else edits the .md file on disk while a collab session is live, we have a classic local-first conflict problem. Need a merge strategy.
3. **Markdown round-tripping under collab**: Yjs operates on the ProseMirror document tree, not the Markdown string. Serialization to .md happens at save time. Two users with slightly different serialization preferences (e.g. ATX vs setext headings) could produce diff noise.
4. **Auth / identity / presence**: who is the other user? Needs some notion of identity.
5. **Why this matters for the product**: collab is probably the natural meeting point between OpenMarkdownReader and noos / IdeaFlow / Thoughtstream. Revisit when those products' roadmaps are clearer.

**Prerequisites**:
- markdown-reader-r1r (Milkdown editor) must ship first.

**Do not work on this without explicit user green-light.** It's here to preserve context, not as a signal to build.

title: More aggressive, visible build number — increment per build, surface in version label
description:
**Problem**: Two builds of the same commit get the SAME build number, because scripts/generate-build-info.js currently sets buildNumber = 'git rev-list --count HEAD' (commit count, not build count). Worse, the build number is hidden inside the About dialog — the user-facing version label just says 'v1.0.4', so the user can't tell at a glance whether they're running an old install vs a fresh build. This bit us today: an install from Dec 26 2025 and a working-tree-with-uncommitted-fix both display as v1.0.4.

**Goal**:
1. **Per-build increment** — every invocation of generate-build-info.js bumps the build number, even on the same commit. Current 'git rev-list --count HEAD' approach is too coarse.
2. **Visible in the version label**, not just the About modal. Render as something like 'v1.0.4.128' or 'v1.0.4 (b128)' wherever the version is shown to the user (status bar, window title, sidebar footer, tab tooltips).
3. **Git hash stays** but as secondary info — the build number is the primary 'are you on the latest?' signal.

**Implementation options for the increment**:
- **A. Persistent counter in build-info.json**: read existing buildNumber, increment by 1, write back. Simple, monotonic. Risk: counter file conflicts in CI / multiple machines. Mitigation: store on a server-side gitignored file or accept per-machine drift.
- **B. Epoch seconds since v1.0.0 release**: like the MAS build does (date +%s). Always unique, always increasing, machine-independent. Less human-friendly (huge numbers).
- **C. Combined**: 'gitCount + .build N' where N is per-machine, e.g., '128.7' meaning 'commit 128, 7th local build of that commit'.

**Recommendation**: Option A for the local file, Option B for MAS (already done). Track the counter in build-info.json and gitignore it.

**Display format**: Render as 'v1.0.4.128' in user-visible labels (status bar, sidebar footer, tab tooltips). Apple constraint: CFBundleVersion is capped at 3 dot-separated integers, so we keep package.json version at '1.0.4' but the display in the JS UI can include the build number as a 4th segment. CFBundleVersion in Info.plist can be set to the build number itself for MAS uniqueness (which is already done).

**Where to surface the new label**:
- Window title (e.g. 'OpenMarkdownReader v1.0.4.128')
- Status bar (already shows nothing? check)
- Sidebar footer (per CLAUDE.md guidance)
- About dialog (already shows it inside)

**Related**: 
- Closed ticket markdown-reader-g8f covers 'auto-increment build number' but it's clearly insufficient for this use case
- markdown-reader-7a5 (closed) is the 'update available' indicator — both should agree on what 'latest version' means
- markdown-reader-jbx (closed) is the manual update check menu

**Acceptance**:
1. Two consecutive 'npm run build:mac' commands on the same commit produce two different build numbers
2. The version label visible to the user without opening About includes the build number
3. Easy to compare two installs at a glance ('I'm on .128, you're on .131')
close_reason: Implemented Option A from ticket: scripts/generate-build-info.js now reads previous buildNumber from build-info.json and uses max(gitCount, previous+1), so each invocation strictly increments. Also made the dev-badge in the titlebar visible on packaged builds (subtle gray pill showing 'b<n>'), giving every install a glance-able build number without opening About. Verified: two consecutive runs produced 130 → 131 on the same commit.

title: Wikilink [[...]] not resolving even with project folder open
description:
**Reproduction**: File /Users/jacobcole/Documents/Main/2023-08-23.md contains [[Todos before BM]]. Project directory is open but the wikilink renders as raw [[...]] text even though a file named `Todos before BM.md` exists.

**Earlier (incorrect) suspicion — RETRACTED**: Case sensitivity is NOT the issue. renderer.js already lowercases both sides of the lookup: index keys at lines 239, 242, and the lookup name at line 274. Ruled out on re-read.

**Real root causes** (three bugs, all must be fixed):

1. **Missing index rebuild on 3 of 5 cache-write sites.** `buildWikiLinkIndex()` was only called at the openDirectory prefetch (renderer.js:1753) and refreshSidebarForExternalMove (renderer.js:3305). These 3 other sites populated `allFilesCache` WITHOUT rebuilding the index:
   - renderer.js:1844 — renderRecentFilesTree fallback fetch
   - renderer.js:1975 — renderRecentFilesTimeline fallback fetch
   - renderer.js:4490 — showCommandPalette fallback fetch

2. **Race condition on the happy path.** In openDirectory, the prefetch at renderer.js:1749 is async, but renderMarkdown → processWikiLinks (renderer.js:3913) is synchronous. If a tab was already open when the folder opened, the file rendered before the index existed, so `processWikiLinks` bailed out at the `size === 0` guard (renderer.js:264/291) and left the `[[...]]` verbatim in innerHTML.

3. **No re-render after index builds.** Once `processWikiLinks` emitted the raw `[[...]]` into markdownBody.innerHTML, nothing invalidated that state when the index later finished loading.

**Fix applied** (renderer.js):
- Added a `setAllFilesCache(files)` helper (~line 261) that assigns the cache, calls `buildWikiLinkIndex()`, AND re-renders the active tab via `renderContent(tab.content, tab.fileName)` if it's a non-editing preview with visible markdownBody. This closes bugs #2 and #3 together.
- Replaced all 5 happy-path `allFilesCache = files` assignments with `setAllFilesCache(files)`:
  - renderer.js:1781 (openDirectory prefetch .then)
  - renderer.js:1872 (renderRecentFilesTree fallback)
  - renderer.js:2003 (renderRecentFilesTimeline fallback)
  - renderer.js:3332 (refreshSidebarForExternalMove)
  - renderer.js:4517 (showCommandPalette fallback)
- Removed the now-redundant explicit `buildWikiLinkIndex()` calls (old lines 1753, 3305).
- Left reset/error paths alone (`allFilesCache = null` / `= []`) — those shouldn't trigger a rebuild.

**Status**: Code change complete, `node --check renderer.js` passes. Ready to verify in app.

**Acceptance**: Opening a folder with a tab already showing [[Todos before BM]] resolves the link to the matching file on first visible paint.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit fbed0e8 (wikilink resolver via setAllFilesCache helper). Verified in renderer.js — all 5 cache assignment sites now call setAllFilesCache which builds the index AND re-renders the active tab.

title: Agent-native control: Unix socket + CLI commands for full programmatic control
Within-tab rich/plain toggle still loses undo on old mode
description:
Phase 2 follow-up to markdown-reader-a4h. Tab switches and preview toggles now preserve undo (per-tab editorEl + easyMDE kept alive), but toggling rich↔plain WITHIN a single tab still loses the OLD mode's undo history because:

- enterRichMode() may call easyMDE.value(textarea.value) to sync, which triggers CM setValue and clears CM history
- leaveRichMode() syncs textarea.value = easyMDE.value(), which resets the textarea's native undo stack

To preserve both stacks across in-tab toggles, we'd need to either (a) re-parent the textarea between the EasyMDEContainer and the bare host without triggering value reassignments, or (b) keep both editors visible/positioned but only one focused and accept duplicate state.

Lower priority than markdown-reader-a4h was — most users switch tabs, fewer toggle modes within a tab. Revisit only if anyone complains.

title: Resolve TestFlight external review rejection for v1.0.4 build 1773489007
description:
**Status**: Both recent MAS builds were rejected for external TestFlight testing:
- Build 1773489007 (v1.0.4) uploaded 2026-03-14, rejected 2026-03-17
- Build 1773226755 (v1.0.4) uploaded 2026-03-11, rejected 2026-03-13

Internal TestFlight works fine (IN_BETA_TESTING on the 'alpha' group). Only external review failed.

**Rejection reason is NOT in the rejection emails** — Apple only puts it in the Resolution Center on App Store Connect. Emails just say 'visit the App Review page'. The API also doesn't expose the rejection text.

**Action required from Jacob**: Open https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/apps/6758376669/testflight/ios, click the rejected build, read the App Review message in the Resolution Center, and paste the reason into this ticket.

**Likely suspects** (to narrow down once we see the reason):
- macOS 26.3 crash (fixed in 6d379bb on 2026-03-14 — upgrade Electron 40.1.0 → 41.0.2). The 1773489007 build was uploaded the same day as the fix commit, so it may or may not contain it.
- Missing beta test notes or reviewer demo info (betaAppReviewDetails shows notes=null, demoAccountRequired=false, but reviewer might need extra context)
- Sandbox entitlement issue
- Helper app signing issue
- Hardcoded paths

**Blocks**: markdown-reader-xwc (Local-Only edition) — it makes no sense to submit a second MAS edition when we can't get the first through beta review.

**Next step after cause identified**: bump to v1.0.5, rebuild with current main.js (which includes Electron 41.0.2, agent-native socket, Unix IPC fixes, and editor-per-tab improvements), upload via scripts/build-mas.sh --upload, resubmit for external review.

comments.author: jacobcole
comments.text:
Resolved the rejection cause and resubmitted:

**Rejection reason** (from Resolution Center): Guideline 2.1(a) — App Completeness. 'We were unable to review the app as it crashed on launch on Mac running macOS 26.3.' (Confirmed via Resolution Center on 2026-04-07; the email notification had no details.)

**Root cause**: The rejected build 1773489007 was uploaded on 2026-03-14 at 04:54 PT. The Electron 40→41 fix for the macOS 26.3 crash was committed 18 minutes later at 05:12 PT (commit 6d379bb). The fix was never rebuilt + re-uploaded — it sat in source for ~3 weeks while Apple rejected the broken build on 2026-03-17 / 2026-03-18.

**Resubmitted 2026-04-07**:
- Build: v1.0.5 (1775607178)
- Electron: 41.0.2 (fix confirmed in package.json)
- Commit: 417356a on feature/daily-notes
- Also includes: agent-native Unix socket, wikilink resolver, editor-per-tab, folder support, and 3 weeks of other improvements
- Added to 'friendsext' external beta group
- Submitted to Apple beta review, state: WAITING_FOR_REVIEW
- Apple typically reviews within 24-48 hours

**Process improvements committed to prevent repeat**:
1. scripts/build-mas.sh now has a Step 0 version safeguard that aborts if package.json version matches the last uploaded build (with --auto-bump flag to auto-increment patch version)
2. scripts/submit-mas-for-external-review.py automates the manual step of adding build to group and submitting for review (previously done by hand in the App Store Connect UI)

**Next**: Monitor beta review state at https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/apps/6758376669/testflight/ios. Close ticket when Apple approves the build or if we need to iterate.

title: Escape key to exit edit mode (auto-save or prompt)
description:
Currently Escape doesn't leave edit mode. Proposal: Escape switches from edit → preview mode.

**Options to consider:**
1. **Auto-save + switch (recommended)**: Escape silently saves any pending changes and switches to preview. Clean, no dialogs, matches Obsidian's Ctrl+E toggle feel. Relies on having autosave or a synchronous save-on-toggle.
2. **Prompt on unsaved**: Escape shows 'Save changes before exiting edit mode?' Save / Discard / Cancel. More explicit but friction.
3. **Toggle only (no save)**: Escape switches mode but leaves the unsaved state as-is — the tab shows the blue dot. Safest, least decision, matches current Cmd+S ergonomics.

**Recommendation**: Option 1 if autosave is reliable; Option 3 otherwise. Avoid option 2 unless the app regularly loses work.

**Also consider**: What if user is in a modal/command palette/search bar when Escape is pressed? Those already consume Escape, so this should only fire when the editor has focus and no overlay is open.

**Reference**: Obsidian uses Ctrl+E to toggle; VS Code has no edit/preview split; Typora auto-saves.
notes: Claimed for implementation. Plan: Escape exits edit mode when the editor has focus and no overlay is open, preserving unsaved state rather than reverting or silently saving.
close_reason: Implemented Escape-to-preview behavior for active editor focus. Escape now exits edit mode without forcing a revert or silent save, preserving dirty state and using the same preview transition path as the explicit toggle.

title: File-tree labels overflow right edge of sidebar (esp. sample Obsidian vault)
description:
## Symptom
Long filenames — especially in sample Obsidian Vault files — overflow past the right edge of the left sidebar instead of being truncated or wrapping.

## Repro (needs confirmation)
1. Open the bundled sample Obsidian vault
2. Notice one or more file-tree entries whose title visibly bleeds past the sidebar's right border
3. Probably worse when the sidebar is at default width and worse on files deep in a nested folder (indent eats horizontal space)

## Suspected cause
Possibly one or more of:
- Missing/insufficient `overflow: hidden` or `text-overflow: ellipsis` on `.file-name` / tree-node label CSS
- A flex layout where the label has `flex-shrink: 0` or `min-width` blocking truncation
- Wiki-link rendering extension recently added (commit `207b6c4`) — worth checking whether it changed tree rendering too, or only document rendering
- Obsidian titles with spaces and unicode may be computed-width differently than ASCII-only

## Investigation checklist
- [ ] Open sample vault, screenshot the overflow in both collapsed and expanded states
- [ ] Inspect offending node in DevTools; note computed `width`, `overflow`, `text-overflow`, `flex` on each ancestor up to the sidebar container
- [ ] Check styles.css rules for `.file-name`, `.file-tree-item`, `.file-tree-label`, etc.
- [ ] Verify the same filename in a non-sample folder — is it Obsidian-specific or any long name?
- [ ] Test edge cases: emoji in name, CJK characters, very long unbroken strings (no spaces)

## Fix (likely)
Add the standard text-truncation pattern to the label element:
```css
.file-tree-label {
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  white-space: nowrap;
  min-width: 0; /* crucial: allows flex children to shrink below content size */
}
```
Plus ensure the parent flex row has `min-width: 0` too (common flex gotcha).

## Bonus
Once truncation works, add a tooltip (`title` attribute or custom) showing the full filename on hover so users can still read long names.
notes: Claimed for implementation. Plan: fix sidebar row flex/truncation so long file/folder labels ellipsize within the sidebar and keep full path/name available on hover.
close_reason: Fixed sidebar tree row truncation by giving rows and labels proper flex shrink/min-width behavior, adding explicit label elements, and preserving full path/name tooltips through render and rename flows.

title: Support Obsidian-style wiki links within opened folders
description:
When a folder is opened in OpenMarkdownReader, support Obsidian-style [[wiki links]]:

**Behavior:**
- Parse `[[page name]]` syntax in markdown files
- Resolve links by matching against filenames in the opened folder (without .md extension)
- Click on wiki link → open that file in a new tab (or switch to existing tab)

- Show a warning/toast when a conflict is detected
- Could potentially show disambiguation UI in future

- Links are relative to the folder root
- Support both `[[page]]` and `[[page|display text]]` syntax

- Broken link highlighting (links to non-existent pages)

title: Smart dev watcher: renderer reload vs full app restart
description:
**Goal**: Make the dev iteration loop dramatically faster by differentiating which source files changed.

**Today**: main.js:3332-3353 watches all source files and unconditionally fires 'source-code-changed' which triggers a full app.relaunch(). That's slow (~3-5s) and loses BrowserWindow state, sidebar collapse state, scroll position, etc.

**Proposal**:
- main.js / preload.js / build-info.json change → full app.relaunch() (must — main process is stale)
- renderer.js / index.html / styles.css / vendor/* change → webContents.reload() per window (much faster, preserves BrowserWindow + main state)

**Implementation sketch** (main.js:3332-3353):
1. Group sourceFiles into 'mainProcessFiles' and 'rendererFiles'
2. fs.watch callback inspects filename, sends either 'source-code-changed' (existing, full restart) or 'renderer-changed' (new event, soft reload)
3. Renderer 'renderer-changed' handler shows the existing pulsing button but with text 'Reload' instead of 'Restart' and triggers webContents.reload() via a new IPC handler
4. Main process IPC handler 'reload-renderer' calls win.webContents.reload() on focused window (or all windows)

**Acceptance**:
- Editing only renderer.js shows 'Reload' button → click reloads in <500ms, preserves window
- Editing main.js shows 'Restart' button → click full restarts as today
- Toast confirms which mode fired

**Related**: markdown-reader-788 (closed) shipped the basic restart button this builds on.
close_reason: Implemented in working tree (not yet committed). Smart dev watcher: main.js:3332-3380. Restart accelerator: main.js:1006-1013. DEV badge tooltip: renderer.js:6421-6448. New IPC: preload.js, main.js reload-renderer handler. User to review + commit.

title: WYSIWYG edit mode that renders markdown inline
description:
**Goal**: Add a Milkdown-based WYSIWYG editor as a new editing mode. Long-term, this could become the default edit mode, with a separate read-only viewing mode preserved.

**Why Milkdown (not Tiptap)**:
- Built on ProseMirror, like Tiptap, but Markdown is first-class (uses remark) so round-tripping text → tree → text is clean
- Plugin-based so we can add only what we need
- Collab path is still open via @milkdown/plugin-collab (see parking ticket markdown-reader-[collab])

**Scope v1**:
- Ship Milkdown as an **alternative** edit mode behind a toggle / setting — do NOT make it default on day one
- Preserve current plain-textarea and EasyMDE modes until Milkdown is proven stable on real notes
- Roundtrip fidelity test: open each file in the user's vault, edit nothing, save, diff — must be zero-change
- Handle wikilinks [[...]] as a custom node (otherwise they get mangled)
- Handle frontmatter passthrough

**Scope v2** (after stability):
- Promote Milkdown to default editing mode
- Keep a 'Plain / Raw Markdown' mode in the View menu for power users and for cases where Milkdown struggles
- Read-only preview mode stays untouched

**Blockers / prerequisites**:
- markdown-reader-a4h (undo persistence across mode/tab switches). A WYSIWYG editor is a much bigger state machine and swapping it in/out while preserving undo is hard. Don't start until editor-per-tab lifecycle is solid.

**Open questions**:
- Do we ship Milkdown via CDN/vendor or bundle through a proper build step? (likely need a build step — Milkdown isn't a single-file drop-in like EasyMDE)
- How does it interact with the custom wikilink indexer and the rich-toolbar toggle?
- Styling parity with current preview CSS

**Merged from**: markdown-reader-pvi (closed as duplicate)

comments.author: jacobcole
comments.text:
## Research Notes

**Options for WYSIWYG/live-preview edit mode:**

| Approach | Raw Text Visible | Rendered Inline | Effort |
|----------|------------------|-----------------|--------|
| EasyMDE (current) | Yes | No | N/A |
| Milkdown/Tiptap | No | Yes (pure WYSIWYG) | Medium |
| Obsidian-style (CodeMirror 6) | Yes | Yes (decorations) | High |
| Split pane (EasyMDE built-in) | Yes | Side-by-side | Already available |

**Recommendations:**
- Milkdown is pure WYSIWYG - hides raw markdown, shows rendered output only
- Obsidian approach is best of both worlds (see raw syntax but styled) - requires CodeMirror 6 with custom decorations
- EasyMDE already has side-by-side mode (columns icon in Rich toolbar)

**Libraries to consider:**
- Milkdown (ProseMirror-based, ~40kb gzipped)
- Tiptap (ProseMirror-based, very flexible)
- CodeMirror 6 with markdown decorations (what Obsidian uses)

title: Ship 'OpenMarkdownReader Local-Only' as a second MAS edition
description:
**Goal**: Ship a second edition of OpenMarkdownReader on the Mac App Store aimed at enterprise users (Anthropic, banks, law firms, anyone with data-exfiltration policies). The killer positioning: the only markdown app on the Mac App Store that cannot leak your documents.

**Product decisions** (finalized 2026-04-07):
- **Name**: "OpenMarkdownReader Local-Only" (NOT "Secure" or "Private" — those would imply the standard edition isn't, which is a bad marketing signal)
- **Distribution**: Mac App Store only (both editions live side-by-side on MAS)
- **Standard edition**: keeps Share button, Report Issue, Noos feedback widget, auto-updater (and continues to ship as direct-download DMG/ZIP too)
- **Local-Only edition**: MAS-only, sandboxed, ZERO network egress for document data

**Two MAS listings from one codebase**:

| | Standard | Local-Only |
|---|---|---|
| Distribution | MAS + direct DMG/ZIP | MAS only |
| Bundle ID | `com.jacobcole.openmarkdownreader` | `com.jacobcole.openmarkdownreader.localonly` |
| productName | OpenMarkdownReader | OpenMarkdownReader Local-Only |
| Share-to-web button | ✅ | ❌ hidden |
| Report Issue menu | ✅ | ❌ hidden |
| Noos feedback widget | ✅ | ❌ not loaded |
| Auto-updater | ✅ (direct build) / managed by MAS | managed by MAS |
| `network.client` entitlement | ✅ | ❌ removed — sandbox physically blocks all outbound connections |
| Icon | standard | variant (TBD — maybe subtle lock/house overlay) |

**Current network egress paths that must be gated off in Local-Only build**:
- `renderer.js:2776` — Share button POSTs document content to `https://globalbr.ai/api/files/anonymous`
- `renderer.js:3116` — Report Issue modal POSTs to `https://globalbr.ai/api/nodes/anonymous-submit`
- `vendor/noos-feedback.js` + `index.html:457` — Noos feedback widget (GET on open, POST on send)
- Auto-updater (already gated via `isMASBuild()`)

**Architecture** (decided):

Single codebase, three layers of enforcement:

1. **Build-edition constant** (`build-edition.js`, generated at build time from `BUILD_EDITION` env var):
   ```js
   module.exports = {
     edition: 'standard' | 'local-only',
     hasCloudShare: boolean,
     hasReportIssue: boolean,
     hasNoosWidget: boolean,
     productName: string,
     bundleId: string,
   };
   ```
   Every network-feature call site checks this constant and hides/disables UI.

2. **Build-time file exclusion** — electron-builder `files` pattern drops `vendor/noos-feedback.js` from local-only builds. Script tag in `index.html` becomes a 404 at runtime, so even if gating breaks, no code to execute.

3. **Entitlement removal** — local-only `entitlements.mas.plist` removes `com.apple.security.network.client`. Apple's sandbox itself blocks every outbound connection. This makes "local only" auditable: a security researcher can verify it with `codesign -d --entitlements - OpenMarkdownReader\ Local-Only.app`.

**Build commands**:
```bash
npm run build:mac              # Standard universal DMG/ZIP (unchanged)
npm run build:mas              # Standard edition on MAS (unchanged)
npm run build:mas-local-only   # Local-Only edition on MAS (new)
```

**Acceptance criteria**:
1. Both MAS editions share >95% of source code; only divergence is the `BUILD_EDITION` constant, electron-builder config, and entitlements.
2. `npm run build:mas-local-only` produces a MAS package with:
   - No `noos-feedback.js` in the bundle
   - No `network.client` entitlement (verifiable via `codesign -d --entitlements -`)
   - Share button hidden, Report Issue menu item hidden
3. Static verification: `grep -r globalbr.ai` in the built app bundle finds nothing in the renderer code for the local-only edition.
4. About dialog on Local-Only shows: "OpenMarkdownReader Local-Only — Sandboxed, no network". Standard About dialog is unchanged.
5. Both editions can be installed simultaneously (different bundle IDs) and share no state.
6. Marketing copy for Local-Only MAS listing emphasizes: "No telemetry. No cloud sync. No share-to-web. Your documents never leave your Mac. Verified by Apple's sandbox — we physically cannot send your data anywhere."

**Implementation plan** (when ready):
1. Create `build-edition.js` generator script (similar to `scripts/generate-build-info.js`)
2. Add `build-edition` import to `main.js` and `renderer.js`
3. Gate all three network paths in `renderer.js` behind `buildEdition.hasCloudShare` etc.
4. Add `package.json` build variant with `mac-local-only` config (different `files`, `mac.appId`, `productName`, `mas.entitlements`)
5. Create `build/entitlements.mas.local-only.plist` (copy of current without `network.client`)
6. Create `scripts/build-mas-local-only.sh` (variant of current `build-mas.sh`)
7. Add "Local-Only edition — Sandboxed, no network" badge in About dialog
8. Update README.md with both editions
9. Create second App Store Connect listing for the new bundle ID
10. Test: install both on same Mac, confirm no network calls from local-only via `tcpdump` / Little Snitch

**Open questions** (remaining):
- Should we ship Local-Only as the FIRST MAS release and hold Standard back until we have both? Or ship Standard first and add Local-Only later?
- Icon variant design — subtle lock overlay? Different color? Or same icon?
- Do we use a shared README or two separate App Store Connect descriptions?

**Related tickets**:
- markdown-reader-il6 (agent-native Unix socket — must work in both editions since it's local IPC)
- markdown-reader-ztt (audit MAS sandbox-unsafe paths — informs gating)
- markdown-reader-st8 (browsing history — must be local-only storage in both editions)

title: Audit MAS build for sandbox-unsafe code paths + surface silent failures
description:
**Context**: MAS build (markdown-reader-il6 uses isMASBuild() gating) runs inside Apple's sandbox with entitlements: app-sandbox, files.user-selected.read-write, files.downloads.read-write, network.client. Anything writing outside those scopes silently fails in MAS but works fine in the notarized build.

**Goal**: Audit renderer.js and main.js for file-system, shell, and IPC operations that could silently fail under sandbox, and either (a) gate them off with isMASBuild() checks or (b) surface a clear error toast to the user when they fail.

**Suspects to review**:
- Recent files persistence / security-scoped bookmarks — do we reestablish bookmarks on relaunch so recents still work in MAS?
- Exports (PDF/HTML/DOCX) that write outside Downloads or user-selected paths
- Backup / auto-save paths writing to arbitrary locations
- Any fs.writeFile / fs.writeFileSync outside known-good sandbox scopes
- Any child_process / spawn / exec calls (shell access is not granted by default)
- open-in-external-editor flows, 'Reveal in Finder' (should work via shell.showItemInFolder)
- Global settings / config writes (should go through app.getPath('userData'))
- Log file writes (electron-log should use a sandbox-safe path by default, verify)
- Update check / download paths (already gated, but worth double-checking)
- CLI-install flow (already gated correctly)

**Acceptance**:
1. Audit document listing every fs/shell/exec call and whether it is safe under sandbox
2. Each unsafe path is either gated with isMASBuild() or wrapped to show a toast/error on failure
3. About dialog shows a 'Sandboxed build' badge so the user can tell at a glance (separate from the existing MAS build: true/false line in the debug info)
4. No silent failures: every file write under sandbox either succeeds or produces a user-visible error

**Related**: Not a blocker on anything, but worth doing before the next MAS release since v1.0.1 had signing issues that masked whether feature parity was actually there.
